generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor for environmental perception and obstacle detection in autonomous vehicle operations. Provides 3D spatial mapping with high precision for point cloud data generation."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelRealSenseD435iCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Intel RealSense D435i depth camera providing RGB-D data for object recognition and scene understanding in autonomous vehicle environments."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CameraRGBDData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Onboard sensors capturing real-time vehicle state information including motor actuation states, steering servo states, and adaptive cruise control parameters."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateTelemetryData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Raw point cloud data from Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor, represented as individual points in a 2D plane for environmental perception."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance LiDARObjectTracksData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks generated from raw LiDAR point cloud data through clustering and tracking algorithms, providing actionable environmental information for autonomous navigation."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
]

instance CameraRGBDData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "RGB-D data from Intel RealSense D435i camera providing color and depth information for enhanced scene understanding and object recognition."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelRealSenseD435iCamera
]

instance VehicleStateTelemetryData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Real-time telemetry data from vehicle including motor actuation states, steering servo states, and adaptive cruise control parameters."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalDataForACC : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    base:desc "Projected driving path and object tracks data derived from sensor fusion, used for Adaptive Cruise Control applications."
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelRealSenseD435iCamera
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudPreprocessingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Data preprocessing model for cleaning, filtering, and structuring raw LiDAR point cloud data before clustering and tracking."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LiDARDataPreprocessingEnabler
]

instance ObjectClusteringAndTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model implementing clustering and tracking algorithms to convert raw LiDAR point cloud data into actionable object tracks for autonomous navigation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ObjectTrackingEnabler
]

instance VehicleStateProcessingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model processing raw vehicle state sensor data to extract structured telemetry information for control and visualization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleStateProcessingEnabler
]

instance VisualizationPreprocessingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Model preparing data for 3D visualization in the Presentation Functional Entity, ensuring proper formatting and synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationPreprocessingEnabler
]

instance RawLiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Raw point cloud data collected from Hokuyo UST-10LX LiDAR sensor before any preprocessing."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo LiDARDataPreprocessingEnabler
]

instance ProcessedLiDARObjectTracks : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed object tracks derived from raw LiDAR point cloud data through clustering and tracking algorithms."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARObjectTracksData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ObjectTrackingEnabler
]

instance ProcessedVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured vehicle state information including motor actuation, steering servo states, and adaptive cruise control parameters."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleStateTelemetryData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VehicleStateProcessingEnabler
]

instance ACCEnvironmentalData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Processed environmental data including projected driving path and object tracks for Adaptive Cruise Control applications."
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalDataForACC
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ACCPathPlanningEnabler
]

instance VisualizationReadyData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Data preprocessed and formatted for real-time 3D visualization of vehicle and environment."
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProcessedLiDARObjectTracks, ProcessedVehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo VisualizationPreprocessingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance LiDARDataPreprocessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity performing preprocessing on raw LiDAR point cloud data including cleaning, filtering, and structuring before clustering and tracking."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataPreprocessingService
]

instance ObjectTrackingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity implementing clustering and tracking algorithms to convert raw LiDAR data into actionable object tracks for autonomous navigation."
    DTDFVocab:enables ObjectTrackingService
]

instance VehicleStateProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity processing raw vehicle state sensor data to extract structured telemetry information for control and visualization."
    DTDFVocab:enables VehicleStateProcessingService
]

instance VisualizationPreprocessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity preparing data for 3D visualization including formatting and synchronization of environmental and vehicle state data."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity serializing data, aligning message types, and performing unit conversions to ensure consistency across all data streams (ISO 23247 compliant)."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTranslationService
]

instance ROS2CommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2 network facilitating seamless integration of processed data between software components including Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking, Controlling FE, and Presentation FE."
    DTDFVocab:enables ROS2CommunicationService
]

instance WiFiUDPCommunicationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Wi-Fi network using UDP protocol for real-time communication between Physical Twin Entity (sensors/vehicle) and Digital Twin Entity (software components)."
    DTDFVocab:enables PhysicalToVirtualCommunicationService
]

instance ACCPathPlanningEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional Entity implementing Adaptive Cruise Control path planning algorithms for dynamic obstacle avoidance and navigation."
    DTDFVocab:enables ACCPathPlanningService
]

// C6: Services
instance DataPreprocessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service handling preprocessing of raw LiDAR point cloud data including cleaning, filtering, and structuring before further processing."
    DTDFVocab:provides LiDARDataPreprocessedInsight
]

instance ObjectTrackingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time object tracking from processed LiDAR data for autonomous navigation and obstacle avoidance."
    DTDFVocab:provides ObjectTrackVisualizationInsight, CollisionAvoidanceAction
]

instance VehicleStateProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service processing raw vehicle state data to provide structured telemetry for control and monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:provides VehicleStateMonitoringInsight
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing real-time 3D visualization of vehicle and environment using processed sensor and state data."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnvironmentVisualizationInsight
]

instance DataTranslationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service ensuring data consistency across all streams through serialization, message alignment, and unit conversion."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataConsistencyInsight
]

instance ROS2CommunicationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service facilitating intra-system communication between software components using ROS2 publish-subscribe model."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComponentCommunicationInsight
]

instance PhysicalToVirtualCommunicationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling real-time data transmission between physical sensors/vehicle and digital twin components using Wi-Fi/UDP."
    DTDFVocab:provides PhysicalToVirtualDataFlowInsight
]

instance ACCPathPlanningService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service implementing Adaptive Cruise Control path planning with dynamic obstacle avoidance and navigation."
    DTDFVocab:provides SafePathPlanningInsight, EmergencyBrakingAction
]

instance CloudEdgeHybridProcessingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling computation offloading between edge (Jetson Orin) and cloud servers for heavy computations like ACC path planning."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComputationOffloadingInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service enabling remote control of vehicle including emergency braking, path re-planning, and sensor recalibration."
    DTDFVocab:provides RemoteControlAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance LiDARDataPreprocessedInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing cleaned and structured LiDAR point cloud data ready for object tracking and environmental perception."
]

instance ObjectTrackVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight showing real-time object tracks derived from processed LiDAR data for navigation and obstacle avoidance."
]

instance CollisionAvoidanceAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action triggering collision avoidance maneuvers based on real-time object tracking data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance VehicleStateMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing real-time monitoring of vehicle state including motor actuation, steering servo states, and adaptive cruise control parameters."
]

instance EnvironmentVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight showing real-time 3D visualization of vehicle and surrounding environment for monitoring and control."
]

instance DataConsistencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight ensuring all data streams maintain consistency through proper serialization, message alignment, and unit conversion."
]

instance ComponentCommunicationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight verifying seamless communication between software components using ROS2 publish-subscribe model."
]

instance PhysicalToVirtualDataFlowInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight monitoring real-time data flow between physical sensors/vehicle and digital twin components."
]

instance SafePathPlanningInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight providing dynamically generated safe paths for Adaptive Cruise Control with obstacle avoidance."
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action triggering emergency braking when potential collisions are detected."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ComputationOffloadingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight showing current computation distribution between edge and cloud processing nodes."
]

instance RemoteControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Manual action enabling remote control of vehicle including emergency braking, path re-planning, and sensor recalibration."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance VedderElectronicSpeedController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Electronic Speed Controller converting desired speed inputs into motor voltage commands for vehicle propulsion, operating under real-time constraints."
]

instance VehicleSteeringController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering control mechanism adjusting vehicle trajectory based on path planning commands from higher-level DT entities."
]

// C1: Physical Twin Entity (Environment)
instance AutonomousVehiclePhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "1/10th-scale autonomous vehicle based on Traxxas teleoperated vehicle platform with NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano compute module for cyber-biophysical system experimentation."
    base:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelRealSenseD435iCamera, VehicleStateSensor,
                VedderElectronicSpeedController, VehicleSteeringController
]

// C1: Digital Twin Entity (Environment)
instance AutonomousVehicleDigitalTwin : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital representation of the autonomous vehicle system managing data preprocessing, actuation, and device communication with responsibilities scoped to data-centric control mechanisms."
    base:contains LiDARDataPreprocessingEnabler, ObjectTrackingEnabler, VehicleStateProcessingEnabler,
                VisualizationPreprocessingEnabler, DataTranslationEnabler, ROS2CommunicationEnabler,
                WiFiUDPCommunicationEnabler, ACCPathPlanningEnabler
]

// C1: User Entity (Environment)
instance AutonomousVehicleUserInterface : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "User interface containing 3D Visualization and Control Panel subcomponents for monitoring and controlling the autonomous vehicle system."
    base:contains VisualizationService, RemoteManagementService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin System (DTS) involves control flows from the **Digital Twin Entity** to the **Physical Twin Entity**, with the following technical specifics:

1. **Command Types**:
   - **Actuation Commands**: Control signals for motor actuation, steering servo actuation, and other vehicle control systems.
   - **Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) Commands**: Generated by the *Controlling FE* to adjust vehicle speed or braking based on real-time data (e.g., object tracks, projected driving path).

2. **Control Message Formats**:
   - **Standardized via Data Translation**: The *Data Translation* component ensures control messages are serialized, aligned with message types, and undergo unit conversions to maintain consistency.
   - **ROS2 Message Formats**: Control signals are transmitted within the ROS2 network, adhering to ROS2 message conventions for interoperability.

3. **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**:
   - **Not explicitly detailed in the document**, but implied to be handled by the *Data Translation* component to ensure message integrity and compatibility.
   - **Legacy System Integration**: The *Data Translation* component may include mechanisms to validate and authenticate control signals for compatibility with existing systems (e.g., *Legacy SW* integration).

4. **Feedback Systems**:
   - **Real-Time Monitoring**: The *Data Monitoring* service provides feedback by displaying vehicle state, object tracks, and projected driving path in the 3D visualization (Fig. 3a).
   - **Closed-Loop Control**: The *Controlling FE* (e.g., ACC) uses feedback from the physical twin (e.g., vehicle state, sensor data) to adjust control commands dynamically.

5. **Conflict Resolution**:
   - **Not explicitly detailed in the document**, but likely managed by the *Controlling FE* or *Data Translation* component to prioritize critical commands (e.g., emergency braking over ACC adjustments).

6. **Command Execution Confirmation**:
   - **Implicit via Feedback Loop**: The system relies on real-time data monitoring to confirm command execution (e.g., verifying motor actuation or steering servo response via updated vehicle state data).
   - **ROS2 Network Reliability**: The ROS2 network ensures timely delivery of control commands, with potential acknowledgment mechanisms (not explicitly described).

The control flow is tightly coupled with the *Actuation FE*, which translates digital control signals into physical actions (e.g., motor/steering servo movements) on the vehicle.
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin System (DTS) enforces a **100 ms time synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components to enable computation offloading and coordination among devices. This temporal granularity is dictated by hardware limitations, though stricter synchronization (sub-100 ms) is mandated by safety-critical vehicular standards (e.g., ISO 23247). The system leverages **clock synchronization protocols** (unspecified but implied to meet the 100 ms requirement) to align time across distributed hardware components (Functional Entities/FEs) deployed on the vehicle. Latency requirements are implicitly tied to real-time data exchange between the **Physical Twin Entity** (sensors/actuators), **Digital Twin Entity** (data management/control), and **User Entity** (visualization/control panel), ensuring deterministic behavior for services like *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DTS orchestrates coordination through a **ROS2-based microservices architecture**, where each **Functional Entity (FE)** is implemented as an independent ROS2 package. Key orchestration mechanisms include:
  - **Entity-Based Mapping**: FEs are grouped into **three hardware-deployed entities** (Physical Twin, Digital Twin, User), with ownership and engineering processes deviating from ISO 23247 to accommodate general automotive functionality (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*).
  - **Component Integration**: ROS2’s **node-based communication** (publish-subscribe topics, services) enables loose coupling between FEs, while **clock synchronization** (≤100 ms) ensures temporal coherence for real-time services.
  - **Resource Management**: The **Physical Twin Entity** acts as a resource hub, hosting both hardware-specific (sensors/actuators) and general-purpose logic, requiring cross-entity coordination via ROS2’s middleware. The *User Interface FE*’s split into visualization/control panels demonstrates **modular orchestration** for user-facing services.
  - **System-Wide Coordination**: Deviations from ISO 23247 (e.g., Physical Twin Entity’s expanded scope) necessitate **explicit ownership definitions** and engineering process adaptations to maintain consistency across distributed components."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The ISO 23247 standard does not explicitly detail **model fidelity requirements**, **validation/verification methods**, or **uncertainty quantification** for the Digital Twin system. However, the **entity-based architecture** implies that **Functional Entities (FEs)** must ensure **consistency** between the digital representation (DTE) and the physical Observable Manufacturing Element (OME). The **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**’s role in real-time data acquisition suggests a reliance on **high-fidelity data** to maintain model accuracy, but no **error detection/correction mechanisms** or **quality assurance processes** are specified. The standard emphasizes **interoperability** and **modularity** but leaves implementation-specific validation methodologies (e.g., simulation vs. empirical testing) undefined. No mention of **uncertainty sources** (e.g., sensor noise, model approximations) or **quantification techniques** (e.g., probabilistic models, Bayesian inference) is provided."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is designed with the following specifications:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **Intra-Entity (Software Components)**: **ROS2 (Robot Operating System 2)** network for low-latency, high-bandwidth communication between software components (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*, *Controlling FE*, *Presentation FE*).
   - **Inter-Entity (Physical-Digital)**: **Wi-Fi network using UDP** in the transport layer for communication between the **Physical Twin Entity** (sensors/vehicle) and **Digital Twin Entity** (software components).

2. **Network Topology and Architecture**:
   - **Hierarchical Model**:
     - **Physical Twin Entity**: Sensors (LiDAR, motor, steering, servo) and vehicle state data sources.
     - **Digital Twin Entity**: Software components (e.g., *Data Pre-Processing FE*, *Controlling FE*, *Presentation FE*) hosted on a computing platform.
     - **User Entity**: Interfaces (e.g., 3D Visualization, Control Panel) for human interaction.
   - **Data Flow**: Sensor data propagates from the Physical Twin to the Digital Twin via Wi-Fi/UDP, while control signals propagate back via the same or a dedicated channel (not explicitly specified).

3. **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**:
   - **Real-Time Constraints**: Critical for ACC applications, where latency must be minimized to ensure timely response to dynamic driving conditions.
   - **UDP for Low Latency**: Wi-Fi/UDP is preferred for its low overhead, though reliability mechanisms (e.g., retransmission) may be implied but not detailed.
   - **ROS2 for High Bandwidth**: Supports high-throughput data exchange between software components (e.g., point cloud processing, visualization).

4. **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**:
   - **Data Translation**: Acts as a central reliability layer by serializing, aligning, and converting data to ensure consistency across streams.
   - **ROS2 Network**: May include built-in reliability features (e.g., QoS policies for real-time systems), though specifics are not provided.
   - **Not Explicitly Detailed**: Redundancy mechanisms (e.g., backup sensors, failover paths) are not mentioned in the document.

5. **Security Protocols for Network Communication**:
   - **Not in Document**: No explicit mention of encryption, authentication, or access control mechanisms for network communication.
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **deployment infrastructure** for this Digital Twin System (DTS) is characterized by the following technical and operational aspects:

1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Edge Deployment**: Implied by the **on-board computing** requirements for real-time processing (e.g., LiDAR clustering, ACC control, 3D visualization).
   - **On-Premise**: The system appears to be vehicle-integrated, with no mention of cloud hosting, suggesting a **localized deployment model** to minimize latency.

2. **Computational and Storage Requirements**:
   - **Computational**:
     - **High-Performance Processing**: Required for real-time LiDAR point cloud clustering and tracking, as well as ACC control logic.
     - **ROS2 Network**: Demands sufficient computational resources to handle inter-component communication and data processing.
   - **Storage**:
     - **Data Storage**: Implicitly supported by the *Data Storage* component in the Digital Twin Entity, though specifics (e.g., capacity, persistence) are not detailed.
     - **Reporting**: The *Reporting FE* suggests storage for generated reports, but no technical details are provided.

3. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - **Not in Document**: No explicit mention of horizontal/vertical scaling, containerization, or modular deployment strategies.

4. **Deployment Models Supported**:
   - **Single-Vehicle Deployment**: The architecture is tailored for a **vehicle-specific Digital Twin**, with no indication of support for multi-vehicle or fleet-wide scaling.
   - **Modular SW Components**: The system is composed of discrete software components (e.g., *Data Pre-Processing FE*, *Controlling FE*), which could theoretically be deployed independently, but no deployment orchestration details are provided.

5. **Availability and Reliability Assurance**:
   - **Real-Time Processing**: Critical for ACC and vehicle control, implying high availability requirements.
   - **No Redundancy Explicitly Stated**: While the *Data Translation* component ensures data consistency, no redundancy mechanisms (e.g., backup components, failover systems) are described.
   - **ROS2 and UDP Reliability**: The use of ROS2 and UDP suggests a focus on low-latency over high reliability, with potential trade-offs in fault tolerance.
"
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to the **ISO 23247 standard for Digital Twin frameworks in manufacturing**, specifically **Part 2: Reference Architecture**, which defines:
  - **Entity-Based Architecture**: A modular design with **four core entities** (OME, Data Collection/Control, Digital Twin, User) and **Functional Entities (FEs)** for granular tasks.
  - **Interoperability Standards**: While the standard does not mandate specific protocols, it aligns with **industrial communication standards** like **OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA)** for data exchange between entities.
  - **Compliance Frameworks**: The architecture ensures **modular compliance** with manufacturing digitalization standards, though **certification requirements** (e.g., ISO 9001, IEC 62443) are not explicitly addressed.
  - **Information Exchange**: Part 4 of ISO 23247 (not detailed here) likely covers **data formats and APIs**, but the reference architecture implies **standardized interfaces** between entities for seamless integration. The system demonstrates **public validation** via a **demonstration link** ([YouTube](https://youtu.be/Rv-sJJQc3WU)), suggesting adherence to **real-world deployment standards**."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system must adhere to **ISO 15622 (Intelligent Transport Systems - Adaptive Cruise Control Systems)** for performance requirements and test procedures, ensuring safety-critical functionality in vehicle-to-everything (V2X) communication. The system respects the **safety-critical nature of the system**, as referenced in the context of **ISO 26262-1 (Road Vehicles – Functional Safety)** standards, though explicit compliance details (e.g., ASIL levels, FMEA, or FTA methodologies) are not specified. Additionally, **3GPP TS 22.185 (Service Requirements for V2X Services)** imposes latency and reliability constraints for real-time data exchange, indirectly influencing safety by ensuring timely system responses. No explicit cybersecurity protocols (e.g., authentication, access control, or threat mitigation strategies) are detailed in the provided documents."
]
